title: phebalium tuberculosum
intro: Phebalium tuberculosum, also known as the rough-leaved phebalium or rough-leaved bush-pea, is a native Australian plant species that belongs to the Rutaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of essential oils such as limonene and geraniol. The health benefits of Phebalium tuberculosum include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
